content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 1, 1993


Brian Libow
City Attorney
City of San Pablo
One Alvarado Square
San Pablo, California  94806

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.  A-93-047

Dear Mr. Libow:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of
January 29, 1993 wherein we discussed your request for advice
Letter No. A-93-047. I informed you that we only issue formal
advice in response to all the facts surrounding a pending
governmental decision.  Since Mayor Gomes has not even accepted
the public relations position with the Chamber of Commerce, and
your questions are general in nature, we cannot issue advice which
would render immunity on issues which are not pending at this
time.

I previously advised you on January 22, 1993 that, in
general, there does not appear to be a "nexus" (as defined in
Regulation 18702.1(d)), which would prevent the mayor from
participating in city council decisions involving individual
businesses which are chamber of Commerce members. Since the mayor
would be employed by the Chamber, the individual business members
are not considered a source of income to him.

However, the Chamber of Commerce, a registered, non-profit
organization, would be a source of income to Mayor Gomes.  Please
consult Regulation 18702.5, which defines material financial
effect for a nonprofit entity source of income indirectly involved
in a decision. You have correctly advised Mayor Gomes that he
must disqualify from participating in any decision in which the
Chamber is directly involved. (Regulation 18702.1).

I trust this answers your questions.

Sincerely,


Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA  95812 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: May a city mayor who accepts employment with the Chamber of Commerce participate in city council decisions involving individual Chamber member businesses, and what recusal requirements apply?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The mayor may generally participate in decisions involving individual Chamber member businesses (no nexus under Regulation 18702.1(d) since members are not his income source), but must disqualify from decisions where the Chamber of Commerce itself is directly involved, as the Chamber would be his income source subject to material financial effect analysis under Regulation 18702.5.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC response confirming prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; advice provided through letter format. Cites specific regulations (18702.1(d) and 18702.5) regarding conflict of interest and recusal requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a city mayor who accepts employment with the Chamber of Commerce participate in city council decisions involving individual Chamber member businesses, and what recusal requirements apply?

CONCLUSION: The mayor may generally participate in decisions involving individual Chamber member businesses (no nexus under Regulation 18702.1(d) since members are not his income source), but must disqualify from decisions where the Chamber of Commerce itself is directly involved, as the Chamber would be his income source subject to material financial effect analysis under Regulation 18702.5.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission February 1, 1993 Brian Libow City Attorney City of San Pablo One Alvarado Square San Pablo, California 94806 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-93-047 Dear Mr. Libow: I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of January 29, 1993 wherein we discussed your request for advice Letter No. A-93-047. I informed you that we only issue formal advice in response to all the facts surrounding a pending governmental decision. Since Mayor Gomes has not even accepted the public relations position with the Chamber of Commerce, and your questions are general in nature, we cannot issue advice which would render immunity on issues which are not pending at this time. I previously advised you on January 22, 1993 that, in general, there does not appear to be a "nexus" (as defined in Regulation 18702.1(d)), which would prevent the mayor from participating in city council decisions involving individual businesses which are chamber of Commerce members. Since the mayor would be employed by the Chamber, the individual business members are not considered a source of income to him. However, the Chamber of Commerce, a registered, non-profit organization, would be a source of income to Mayor Gomes. Please consult Regulation 18702.5, which defines material financial effect for a nonprofit entity source of income indirectly involved in a decision. You have correctly advised Mayor Gomes that he must disqualify from participating in any decision in which the Chamber is directly involved. (Regulation 18702.1). I trust this answers your questions. Sincerely, Jeff Marschner General Counsel By: Jill Stecher Counsel, Legal Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that a San Pablo mayor accepting employment with the Chamber of Commerce may participate in city decisions affecting individual Chamber member businesses but must recuse from decisions directly involving the Chamber as an organization.
